data.text: I'll start by reading the test file to understand the current flaky test.

data.output:
1	package agent
2	
3	import (
4		"bytes"
5		"context"
6		"fmt"
7		"strings"
8		"sync/atomic"
9		"testing"
10		"time"
11	
12		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/agent/executeloop"
13		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/bead"
14		"github.com/DocumentDrivenDX/ddx/internal/config"
15		"github.com/stretchr/testify/assert"
16		"github.com/stretchr/testify/require"
17	)
18	
19	// TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue covers watch mode: the loop must
20	// NOT exit when nextCandidate returns no
21	// eligible bead. It must reset the per-Run attempted/hookFailed maps and
22	// sleep, then poll again. Cancelling the context is the only way out.
23	func TestLoop_StaysAliveWithEmptyQueue(t *testing.T) {
24		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
25		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
26	
27		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
28			Store: store,
29			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
30				t.Fatalf("executor must not run on an empty queue")
31				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
32			}),
33		}
34	
35		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
36		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
37	
38		// Use a tiny poll interval so the test runs quickly; cancel after enough
39		// wall-clock for several empty-poll cycles to confirm the loop is still
40		// running and not bailing out after the first empty poll.
41		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
42		pollInterval := 5 * time.Millisecond
43		cancelAfter := 50 * time.Millisecond
44		go func() {
45			time.Sleep(cancelAfter)
46			cancel()
47		}()
48	
49		start := time.Now()
50		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
51			Mode:         executeloop.ModeWatch,
52			IdleInterval: pollInterval,
53		})
54		elapsed := time.Since(start)
55	
56		// Context cancellation surfaces as the returned error. The key
57		// invariant: the loop survived past the first empty poll.
58		require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
59		require.NotNil(t, result)
60		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts)
61		assert.True(t, elapsed >= cancelAfter,
62			"loop must run until ctx cancellation; ran for %s, expected >= %s", elapsed, cancelAfter)
63	}
64	
65	func TestWorkWatchIdleStdout_PrintsQueueStatusAndHumanBlockers(t *testing.T) {
66		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
67		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
68		seedWatchIdleQueue(t, store)
69	
70		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
71			Store: store,
72			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
73				t.Fatalf("executor must not run on an idle queue")
74				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
75			}),
76			Now: fixedWatchStdoutTime,
77		}
78	
79		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
80		progressCh := make(chan ProgressEvent, 16)
81		done := make(chan struct{})
82		var idleEvents int32
83		go func() {
84			for {
85				select {
86				case evt := <-progressCh:
87					if evt.Phase == "loop.idle" {
88						atomic.AddInt32(&idleEvents, 1)
89						cancel()
90						return
91					}
92				case <-done:
93					return
94				}
95			}
96		}()
97		defer close(done)
98	
99		var logBuf bytes.Buffer
100		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
101		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
102		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
103			Mode:         executeloop.ModeWatch,
104			IdleInterval: 30 * time.Second,
105			Log:          &logBuf,
106			ProgressCh:   progressCh,
107		})
108		require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
109		require.NotNil(t, result)
110		require.NotNil(t, result.QueueSnapshot)
111		assert.Equal(t, 3, result.QueueSnapshot.HumanReviewBlockerCount)
112		assert.Equal(t, 30, result.QueueSnapshot.HumanReviewBlockedTotal)
113		require.Len(t, result.QueueSnapshot.HumanReviewBlockers, 3)
114		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&idleEvents), "watch mode must still emit loop.idle progress")
115	
116		out := logBuf.String()
117		assert.Contains(t, out, "12:34:56 idle: no execution-ready beads; sleeping 30s")
118		assert.Contains(t, out, "queue: execution-ready=0")
119		assert.Contains(t, out, "operator-attention=4") // 3 proposed blockers + 1 standalone proposed bead
120		assert.Contains(t, out, "needs-human/investigation=3")
121		assert.Contains(t, out, "cooldown/deferred=1")
122		assert.Contains(t, out, "next-retry=")
123		assert.Contains(t, out, "execution-ineligible=1")
124		assert.Contains(t, out, "superseded=1")
125		assert.Contains(t, out, "epics=1")
126		assert.Contains(t, out, "epic-closure-candidates=1")
127		assert.Contains(t, out, "30 beads blocked behind 3 needs-human blockers")
128		assert.Contains(t, out, "1. ddx-human-1 Needs human 1 (10 downstream)")
129		assert.Contains(t, out, "2. ddx-human-2 Needs human 2 (10 downstream)")
130		assert.Contains(t, out, "3. ddx-human-3 Needs human 3 (10 downstream)")
131	}
132	
133	func TestWorkWatchIdleStdout_RepeatedPollKeepsCompactQueueStatus(t *testing.T) {
134		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
135		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
136		seedWatchIdleQueue(t, store)
137	
138		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
139			Store: store,
140			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
141				t.Fatalf("executor must not run on an idle queue")
142				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
143			}),
144			Now: fixedWatchStdoutTime,
145		}
146	
147		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
148		wakeCh := make(chan struct{}, 1)
149		progressCh := make(chan ProgressEvent, 16)
150		done := make(chan struct{})
151		var idleEvents int32
152		go func() {
153			for {
154				select {
155				case evt := <-progressCh:
156					if evt.Phase != "loop.idle" {
157						continue
158					}
159					if atomic.AddInt32(&idleEvents, 1) == 1 {
160						wakeCh <- struct{}{}
161					} else {
162						cancel()
163						return
164					}
165				case <-done:
166					return
167				}
168			}
169		}()
170		defer close(done)
171	
172		var logBuf bytes.Buffer
173		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
174		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
175		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
176			Mode:         executeloop.ModeWatch,
177			IdleInterval: time.Hour,
178			Log:          &logBuf,
179			ProgressCh:   progressCh,
180			WakeCh:       wakeCh,
181		})
182		require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
183		require.NotNil(t, result)
184		assert.Equal(t, int32(2), atomic.LoadInt32(&idleEvents))
185	
186		out := logBuf.String()
187		assert.Equal(t, 2, strings.Count(out, "idle: no execution-ready beads; sleeping 1h0m0s"))
188		assert.Equal(t, 2, strings.Count(out, "queue: execution-ready=0"))
189		assert.Equal(t, 2, strings.Count(out, "30 beads blocked behind 3 needs-human blockers"))
190		assert.Equal(t, 1, strings.Count(out, "ddx-human-1 Needs human 1 (10 downstream)"))
191		assert.Equal(t, 1, strings.Count(out, "ddx-human-2 Needs human 2 (10 downstream)"))
192		assert.Equal(t, 1, strings.Count(out, "ddx-human-3 Needs human 3 (10 downstream)"))
193	}
194	
195	func TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle(t *testing.T) {
196		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
197		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
198	
199		ctx, cancel := context.WithCancel(context.Background())
200		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
201			Store: store,
202			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
203				cancel()
204				return ExecuteBeadReport{
205					BeadID:    beadID,
206					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
207					SessionID: "sess-after-idle",
208					ResultRev: "feedface",
209				}, nil
210			}),
211			Now: fixedWatchStdoutTime,
212		}
213	
214		wakeCh := make(chan struct{}, 1)
215		progressCh := make(chan ProgressEvent, 16)
216		done := make(chan struct{})
217		errCh := make(chan error, 1)
218		var activeEvents int32
219		go func() {
220			for {
221				select {
222				case evt := <-progressCh:
223					switch evt.Phase {
224					case "loop.idle":
225						errCh <- store.Create(&bead.Bead{
226							ID:       "ddx-ready-after-idle",
227							Title:    "spec: define full DDx temp cleanup in work cycle",
228							Priority: 0,
229						})
230						wakeCh <- struct{}{}
231					case "loop.active":
232						atomic.AddInt32(&activeEvents, 1)
233					}
234				case <-done:
235					return
236				}
237			}
238		}()
239		defer close(done)
240	
241		var logBuf bytes.Buffer
242		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
243		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
244		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
245			Mode:         executeloop.ModeWatch,
246			IdleInterval: time.Hour,
247			Log:          &logBuf,
248			ProgressCh:   progressCh,
249			WakeCh:       wakeCh,
250		})
251		require.ErrorIs(t, err, context.Canceled)
252		require.NoError(t, <-errCh)
253		require.NotNil(t, result)
254		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&activeEvents), "watch mode must still emit loop.active progress")
255	
256		out := logBuf.String()
257		transition := "taking next ready bead from queue: ddx-ready-after-idle — spec: define full DDx temp cleanup in work cycle"
258		header := "▶ ddx-ready-after-idle: spec: define full DDx temp cleanup in work cycle"
259		assert.Contains(t, out, "\n12:34:56 "+transition+"\n")
260		assert.Contains(t, out, header)
261		assert.Less(t, strings.Index(out, transition), strings.Index(out, header))
262	}
263	
264	// TestDrain_RoutingPreflightRunsOnce covers the bootstrap behavior required
265	// by ddx-848069a3: routing preflight runs once before the drain loop starts
266	// and does not repeat as additional beads are processed.
267	func TestDrain_RoutingPreflightRunsOnce(t *testing.T) {
268		inner, first, second := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
269		store := &claimCountingStore{Store: inner}
270	
271		var executed []string
272		var preflightCalls int32
273		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
274			Store: store,
275			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
276				executed = append(executed, beadID)
277				return ExecuteBeadReport{
278					BeadID:    beadID,
279					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
280					SessionID: "sess-after-preflight",
281					ResultRev: "deadbeef",
282				}, nil
283			}),
284		}
285	
286		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker", Harness: "claude", Model: "gpt-5"}
287		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
288	
289		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
290			Mode: executeloop.ModeDrain,
291			RoutePreflight: func(ctx context.Context, harness, model string) error {
292				atomic.AddInt32(&preflightCalls, 1)
293				return nil
294			},
295		})
296		require.NoError(t, err)
297		require.NotNil(t, result)
298	
299		assert.Equal(t, int32(1), atomic.LoadInt32(&preflightCalls), "preflight must run once at startup")
300		assert.Equal(t, int32(2), atomic.LoadInt32(&store.claimCalls), "both ready beads must still be claimed")
301		require.Len(t, executed, 2, "both ready beads must execute")
302		assert.ElementsMatch(t, []string{first.ID, second.ID}, executed)
303		assert.Equal(t, 2, result.Attempts)
304		assert.Equal(t, 2, result.Successes)
305	
306		gotFirst, err := inner.Get(first.ID)
307		require.NoError(t, err)
308		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, gotFirst.Status)
309		gotSecond, err := inner.Get(second.ID)
310		require.NoError(t, err)
311		assert.Equal(t, bead.StatusClosed, gotSecond.Status)
312	}
313	
314	// TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits covers ddx-dc157075 back-compat: --once must
315	// still terminate the loop after exactly one ready bead is processed, even
316	// when more beads are queue-ready. This guards against an over-eager fix
317	// that drops the Once gate.
318	func TestLoop_OnceFlagStillExits(t *testing.T) {
319		store, _, _ := newExecuteLoopTestStore(t)
320	
321		var executed []string
322		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
323			Store: store,
324			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
325				executed = append(executed, beadID)
326				return ExecuteBeadReport{
327					BeadID:    beadID,
328					Status:    ExecuteBeadStatusSuccess,
329					SessionID: "sess-once",
330					ResultRev: "cafebabe",
331				}, nil
332			}),
333		}
334	
335		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
336		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
337	
338		// Even in watch-capable runtimes, once mode must still cause the loop to
339		// exit after one bead.
340		result, err := worker.Run(context.Background(), rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
341			Mode: executeloop.ModeOnce,
342		})
343		require.NoError(t, err)
344		require.NotNil(t, result)
345	
346		assert.Len(t, executed, 1, "--once must process exactly one bead")
347		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Attempts)
348		assert.Equal(t, 1, result.Successes)
349	}
350	
351	// TestLoop_ExplicitDrainExits covers drain-and-exit semantics: an empty queue
352	// is terminal unless watch mode is selected.
353	func TestLoop_ExplicitDrainExits(t *testing.T) {
354		store := bead.NewStore(t.TempDir())
355		require.NoError(t, store.Init())
356	
357		worker := &ExecuteBeadWorker{
358			Store: store,
359			Executor: ExecuteBeadExecutorFunc(func(ctx context.Context, beadID string) (ExecuteBeadReport, error) {
360				t.Fatalf("executor must not run when queue is empty")
361				return ExecuteBeadReport{}, nil
362			}),
363		}
364	
365		cfgOpts := config.TestLoopConfigOpts{Assignee: "worker"}
366		rcfg := config.NewTestConfigForLoop(cfgOpts).Resolve(config.TestLoopOverrides(cfgOpts))
367	
368		// Drain mode exits when the queue is empty rather than idling. A
369		// bounded-time context guards against a regression that would hang.
370		ctx, cancel := context.WithTimeout(context.Background(), 2*time.Second)
371		defer cancel()
372	
373		start := time.Now()
374		result, err := worker.Run(ctx, rcfg, ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime{
375			Mode: executeloop.ModeDrain,
376		})
377		elapsed := time.Since(start)
378	
379		require.NoError(t, err, "drain mode must return cleanly without timing out")
380		require.NotNil(t, result)
381		assert.True(t, elapsed < time.Second,
382			"explicit poll=0 must exit promptly when queue is empty; took %s", elapsed)
383		assert.True(t, result.NoReadyWork)
384		assert.Equal(t, 0, result.Attempts)
385	}
386	
387	func fixedWatchStdoutTime() time.Time {
388		return time.Date(2026, 5, 9, 12, 34, 56, 789000000, time.UTC)
389	}
390	
391	func seedWatchIdleQueue(t *testing.T, store *bead.Store) {
392		t.Helper()
393	
394		upstream := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-upstream", Title: "External blocker", Priority: 0}
395		require.NoError(t, store.Create(upstream))
396		require.NoError(t, store.UpdateWithLifecycleStatus(upstream.ID, bead.StatusBlocked, bead.LifecycleTransitionOptions{
397			ExternalBlockerReason: "waiting for upstream",
398			Reason:                "test fixture",
399			Source:                "test",
400		}, nil))
401	
402		for i := 1; i <= 3; i++ {
403			blocker := &bead.Bead{
404				ID:       fmt.Sprintf("ddx-human-%d", i),
405				Title:    fmt.Sprintf("Needs human %d", i),
406				Priority: i,
407			}
408			blocker.AddDep(upstream.ID, "blocks")
409			require.NoError(t, store.Create(blocker))
410			require.NoError(t, store.UpdateWithLifecycleStatus(blocker.ID, bead.StatusProposed, bead.LifecycleTransitionOptions{
411				OperatorRequired: true,
412				Reason:           "test fixture: operator attention required",
413				Source:           "test",
414			}, nil))
415	
416			prevID := blocker.ID
417			for n := 1; n <= 10; n++ {
418				downstream := &bead.Bead{
419					ID:       fmt.Sprintf("ddx-down-%d-%02d", i, n),
420					Title:    fmt.Sprintf("Downstream %d %02d", i, n),
421					Priority: 4,
422				}
423				downstream.AddDep(prevID, "blocks")
424				require.NoError(t, store.Create(downstream))
425				prevID = downstream.ID
426			}
427		}
428	
429		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
430			ID:       "ddx-proposed",
431			Title:    "Proposed operator attention",
432			Status:   bead.StatusProposed,
433			Priority: 3,
434		}))
435		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
436			ID:       "ddx-not-eligible",
437			Title:    "Execution ineligible",
438			Priority: 4,
439			Extra:    map[string]any{bead.ExtraExecutionElig: false},
440		}))
441		require.NoError(t, store.Create(&bead.Bead{
442			ID:       "ddx-superseded",
443			Title:    "Superseded",
444			Priority: 4,
445			Extra:    map[string]any{"superseded-by": "ddx-replacement"},
446		}))
447	
448		cooldown := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-cooldown", Title: "Retry later", Priority: 4}
449		require.NoError(t, store.Create(cooldown))
450		// Use time.Now().Add so the cooldown is always in the future regardless of when
451		// the test runs relative to fixedWatchStdoutTime (which is a fixed past date).
452		require.NoError(t, store.SetExecutionCooldown(cooldown.ID, time.Now().Add(24*time.Hour), ExecuteBeadStatusNoChanges, "retry later"))
453	
454		ordinaryEpic := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-epic-open", Title: "Open epic", IssueType: "epic", Priority: 4}
455		ordinaryEpicChild := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-epic-open-child", Title: "Open epic child", Parent: ordinaryEpic.ID, Priority: 4}
456		ordinaryEpicChild.AddDep(upstream.ID, "blocks")
457		require.NoError(t, store.Create(ordinaryEpic))
458		require.NoError(t, store.Create(ordinaryEpicChild))
459	
460		closureEpic := &bead.Bead{ID: "ddx-epic-closure", Title: "Closure epic", IssueType: "epic", Priority: 4}
461		closedChild := &bead.Bead{
462			ID:       "ddx-epic-closed-child",
463			Title:    "Closed epic child",
464			Status:   bead.StatusClosed,
465			Parent:   closureEpic.ID,
466			Priority: 4,
467		}
468		require.NoError(t, store.Create(closureEpic))
469		require.NoError(t, store.Create(closedChild))
470	}
471

data.text: Now let me look at the worker implementation to understand the flow better.

input.description: Find execute bead loop worker Run implementation
input.prompt: In the repository at /var/tmp/ddx-exec-wt/.execute-bead-wt-ddx-dc691319-20260509T224105-6b3cbc16/cli/internal/agent/, find the file that contains the ExecuteBeadWorker.Run implementation and the watch mode idle loop. Specifically look for how the worker emits ProgressEvent with Phase "loop.idle" and then waits for WakeCh. Return the relevant code sections with file paths and line numbers. Search breadth: medium.

473-// ExecuteBeadWorker drains the current single-project execution-ready queue.
474-// It intentionally does not retry a failed/conflicted bead again in the same
475-// process run; a later operator-driven invocation can create the next attempt.

506	// emitProgress sends a ProgressEvent to runtime.ProgressCh non-blocking.
507	// If ch is nil or full the event is silently dropped.
508	func emitProgress(ch chan<- ProgressEvent, evt ProgressEvent) {
509		if ch == nil {
510			return
511		}
512		select {
513		case ch <- evt:
514		default:
515		}
516	}

518	// newProgressEvent builds a ProgressEvent with a random event_id and current timestamp.
519	func newProgressEvent(workerID, projectID, beadID, attemptID, harness, model, profile, phase string, phaseSeq int, heartbeat bool, elapsedMS int64) ProgressEvent {
520		return ProgressEvent{
521			EventID:   "evt-" + randomProgressID(),
522			AttemptID: attemptID,
523			WorkerID:  workerID,
524			ProjectID: projectID,
525			BeadID:    beadID,
526			Harness:   harness,
527			Model:     model,
528			Profile:   profile,
529			Phase:     phase,
530			PhaseSeq:  phaseSeq,
531			Heartbeat: heartbeat,
532			TS:        time.Now().UTC(),
533			ElapsedMS: elapsedMS,
534		}
535	}
536	
537	func randomProgressID() string {
538		return fmt.Sprintf("%x", time.Now().UnixNano())[:8]
539	}

87	func (r ExecuteBeadLoopRuntime) loopIntent() (executeloop.Mode, time.Duration) {
88		mode := r.Mode
89		if mode == "" {
90			if r.Once {
91				mode = executeloop.ModeOnce
92			} else {
93				mode = executeloop.ModeDrain
94			}
95		}
96		idleInterval := r.IdleInterval
97		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
98			idleInterval = r.PollInterval
99		}
100		if idleInterval == 0 && mode == executeloop.ModeWatch {
101			idleInterval = 30 * time.Second
102		}
103		return mode, idleInterval
104	}
105	
106	// DefaultReviewMaxRetries is the number of reviewer attempts allowed per
107	// committed result_rev before the loop emits a terminal
108	// `review-manual-required` event and stops re-executing primary. FEAT-022 §14.
109	const DefaultReviewMaxRetries = 3
110	
111	// MaxLoopCooldown is the absolute upper bound the execute-loop will set for
112	// any execute-loop-retry-after value. Year-scale parks effectively mean
113	// "never retry" and that should be a deliberate operator choice via
114	// `ddx bead update --set execute-loop-retry-after=...`, not an automatic
115	// loop decision. Beyond this cap, the loop refuses to lengthen the cooldown
116	// further; an operator extending it manually still works.
117	const MaxLoopCooldown = 24 * time.Hour
118	
119	// LandConflictCooldown is the short cooldown applied when all conflict-recovery
No matches found

data.text: Now I have a clear picture of the flake. The test runs `worker.Run` in the main goroutine while a background goroutine handles `progressCh` events. Under load, that goroutine can be delayed after receiving `loop.idle`, causing a race with the worker's `sleepOrWake` select. The fix is to invert: run the worker in a goroutine and process progress events synchronously on the main goroutine.

All green. The commit is made. Here's a summary of what was done:

**AC verification:**
1. ✅ `TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle` restructured — replaced the background goroutine with a synchronous main-goroutine select loop that handles `loop.idle` immediately, eliminating the scheduling race
2. ✅ `go test -count=10 -run TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle ./internal/agent/...` — 10 consecutive passes
3. ✅ `CGO_ENABLED=1 go test -count=3 -race -run TestWorkWatchStdout_PrintsNextReadyTransitionAfterIdle ./internal/agent/...` — 3 race-detector passes
4. ✅ `go test ./internal/agent/...` — full package green
5. ✅ `lefthook run pre-commit` — all hooks pass
